channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Built an All-in-One Local AI App — Chat, Image Gen, and Video Gen in One UI
item.description: Last year my desktop looked like a disaster zone. I had an Ollama terminal running in one corner, ComfyUI open in Firefox with like 14 tabs of different workflows, a separate janky video...

item.title: Before the Internet
item.description: Before the Internet Remembering a World Without WiFi It's hard to imagine life before the internet, especially for developers. Our days are shaped by push notifications, cloud deployments, and...

item.title: Stop Writing Tests First — Write Formal Specs. Let AI Agent Teams Build Your System.
item.description: The Problem Nobody's Talking About Everyone's excited about AI-powered coding. GitHub Copilot autocompletes your functions. Claude and GPT-4 generate entire modules from prompts. But here's the...

item.title: Opus 5 Is Coming - Is Your Code Governance Ready?
item.description: Anthropic accidentally leaked details of their most powerful model ever built. The implications for teams using AI coding agents are significant — and most aren't prepared. What We Know About...

item.title: Stop Sending Every PDF Page to a VLM: A Parser-First Document AI Pattern with LiteParse
item.description: Most Document AI teams are overusing VLMs. The default pattern still looks like this: take a PDF send the whole thing to a big multimodal model hope the output is good enough patch the failures...

item.title: Porting Test Drive II from SNES to PC, Part 39: Closing the No-Opponent Handoff Into 02:9016
item.description: This checkpoint closes the last front-end ambiguity in the SNES Select Opponent flow. The fourth slot was already structurally solved as the stopwatch / clock path. What remained open was whether...

item.title: I Stopped Vibe Coding and Started Using Prompt Contracts, Here's What the Data
item.description: You asked Claude Code to build a Supabase auth flow with row-level security. You got a flawless, production-grade Firebase auth system. Technically impressive. Fundamentally wrong. 2,400 lines of...

item.title: STIR/SHAKEN para VICIdial: La Guia Completa de Implementacion 2026

item.title: La Guia Completa de Instalacion de VICIdial (2026): De Servidor Nuevo a Primera Llamada en Menos de 2 Horas
item.description: Ultima actualizacion: Marzo 2026 | Tiempo de lectura: ~18 minutos Ya hiciste las cuentas. Convoso quiere $150/puesto/mes. Five9 quiere aun mas. Mientras tanto, VICIdial — el mismo marcador...

item.title: Nuki’s one-touch retrofit smart lock got its first-ever discount
item.description: The Nuki Smart Lock is the best retrofit smart lock currently available for people who want a minimalist, feature-packed upgrade to their preexisting deadlock. It’s currently selling for $149 at...

item.title: VCs are betting billions on AI’s next wave, so why is OpenAI killing Sora?
item.description: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres...

item.title: This modular crafting machine can create custom shirts, phone cases, and molds
item.description: xTool, a company best known amongst makers for its laser-based cutting and engraving tools, has announced a new multi-function crafting machine called the WonderPress. It's not an all-in-one...

item.title: Apple Still Plans to Sell iPhones When It Turns 100
item.description: As the tech giant turns 50, WIRED spoke to executives about how they plan to win in the AI era.

item.title: Motorola’s Razr Ultra 2026 might be a hair thicker than last year’s version
item.description: While most flagship phones have been steadily getting slimmer over recent years, the upcoming 2026 version of the Motorola Razr Ultra could end up being a bit chunkier than its predecessor. Leaked...

item.title: Meta’s court losses could be just the beginning

item.title: The Download: the internet’s best weather app, and why people freeze their brains
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather...

item.title: Sony is raising PS5 prices by $100 in April
item.description: Sony is raising the price of its PlayStation 5 consoles globally starting April 2nd. In an announcement on Friday, Sony says that the standard PS5 will now cost $649.99, up from $549.99....

item.title: AitM Phishing Targets TikTok Business Accounts Using Cloudflare Turnstile Evasion
item.description: Threat actors are using adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) phishing pages to seize control of TikTok for Business accounts in a new campaign, according to a report from Push Security. Business...

item.title: The Latest AI Documentary Asks: Just How Scared Should We Be?
item.description: The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist seeks the middle ground on a polarizing technology—and ends up letting tech execs like Sam Altman off the hook.

item.title: We Are At War
item.description: Rising geopolitical tensions are reflected (or in some cases preceded) by cyber operations, while technology itself has become politicized. Let’s admit it: we are in the middle of it....

item.title: I Asked ChatGPT 500 Questions. Here Are the Ads I Saw Most Often
item.description: Ads are rolling out across the US on ChatGPT’s free tier. I asked OpenAI's bot 500 questions to see what these ads were like and how they related to my prompts.

item.title: Bearlyfy Hits Russian Firms with Custom GenieLocker Ransomware
item.description: A pro-Ukrainian group called Bearlyfy has been attributed to more than 70 cyber attacks targeting Russian companies since it first surfaced in the threat landscape in January 2025, with recent...
